message:
fix(writing): soften opening claim from universal to "surprising number"

"Almost everyone I know is eventually going to die without a serious
plan" reads as a factual claim that is categorically false. The real
stat (roughly two-thirds of US adults lack a will) is surprising but
not universal. Rephrasing to "A surprising number of the people I
know" keeps the emotional punch while being defensible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -563,9 +563,10 @@ export function WillsEstateArticle() {

- Almost everyone I know is eventually going to die without a serious - plan for what happens to their money, their house, their pets, their - data, or their kids. Some will leave handwritten notes a grieving + A surprising number of the people I know will eventually die + without a serious plan for what happens to their money, their + house, their pets, their data, or their kids. Some will leave + handwritten notes a grieving spouse cannot legally execute. Some will leave a will from 2011 that still names an ex. Some will leave nothing, and a county court will decide for them. This is not because they don't care.
